name: availability: same-day turnover — check-out 10:00 and a new check-in 14:00 the same day on the same room is allowed (B8/F16)
comment: recB0 occupies Room 1 until TODAY 10:00 SAST; the guest checks in TODAY 14:00 SAST and gets that same room. Worth proving on its own — it is the revenue case, and losing it would cost a sellable night on every turnover. NOT an exclusive-bounds proof, despite what the build spec called it: exclusive and inclusive bounds disagree on exactly one input, a check-in instant equal to a check-out instant, and the overnight defaults compare 14:00 against 10:00, so this fixture passes under both definitions. Verified by mutation — flipping rangesOverlap to <=/>= leaves every fixture green. The bounds rule is pinned in test/availability.test.js instead, where the shared instant is reachable. Room 1 is seeded as Cleaning on purpose: a real turnover leaves the room mid-clean, so this doubles as proof that the status allowlist keeps Cleaning rooms sellable.
